title: Siphocampylus foetidus
taxonomy: Siphocampylus foetidus is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae, commonly known as the bellflower family. This family is a large and diverse group of plants, comprising around 2,300 species. Siphocampylus foetidus is native to Central and South America, and its classification is further broken down into the order Asterales, and the clade eurosids I. The Campanulaceae family is primarily composed of annual and perennial herbaceous plants, as well as some woody shrubs and small trees. Siphocampylus foetidus can be further classified at the genus and species level within this family.

intro: Siphocampylus foetidus, also known as the stinking Siphocampylus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Siphocampylus foetidus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
